title: Control Loop Deviation
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:control"
| eval err=abs(pv-sp)
| where err > deadband*5 OR (abs(out)>95 AND err>deadband)
| timechart span=1m avg(err) by loop_id
description: PV diverging from SP with saturated output indicates tuning issues or actuator faults.
value: PV diverging from SP with saturated output indicates tuning issues or actuator faults.
implementation: Define deadband per loop. Alert when sustained error exceeds threshold or output pegs. Integrate with maintenance CMMS.
visualization: Line chart (PV vs SP), Gauge (loop error), Table (loops in alarm).
knownFalsePositives: Similar activity during production startups, control downloads, and approved remote maintenance—triage with the SCADA HMI and change log.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OPC-UA process tags.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="opcua:control"` (pv, sp, out).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Define deadband per loop. Alert when sustained error exceeds threshold or output pegs. Integrate with maintenance CMMS.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="opcua:control"
| eval err=abs(pv-sp)
| where err > deadband*5 OR (abs(out)>95 AND err>deadband)
| timechart span=1m avg(err) by loop_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Control Loop Deviation** — PV diverging from SP with saturated output indicates tuning issues or actuator faults.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="opcua:control"` (pv, sp, out). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OPC-UA process tags. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: opcua:control. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="opcua:control". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **err** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where err > deadband*5 OR (abs(out)>95 AND err>deadband)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1m** buckets with a separate series **by loop_id** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (PV vs SP), Gauge (loop error), Table (loops in alarm).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: control loop deviation before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
